heading: Add Project Access from a Project
body:
To create an API key for your project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas projects apiKeys create [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas projects apiKeys create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-apiKeys-create).

After you create the API (Application Programming Interface) key for your project, use the
Atlas UI to add an API (Application Programming Interface) access list entry. You can't use the
API (Application Programming Interface) key for the project until you set up the API (Application Programming Interface) access list.

> **Note:** 
>
> Atlas CLI Limitation
>
> You can't edit the API (Application Programming Interface) access list for a project API (Application Programming Interface) key
> using the Atlas CLI.

To add an API (Application Programming Interface) access list entry using the Atlas UI:

- Click Add Access List Entry.

- Enter an IP (Internet Protocol) address from which you want Atlas to
  accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this API (Application Programming Interface) Key.
  You can also click Use Current IP Address if the host
  you are using to access Atlas will also make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
  using this API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click Save.
